text.title: Business Model Research Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Business Model Research methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Populate all 9 Canvas blocks before naming any price; tag every cell Hard (sourced) or Soft (founder estimate).
rule.rationale: Agents skip the Canvas and produce unit economics without understanding cost structure; Hard/Soft tagging makes the assumptions ledger auditable.

rule.statement: Shortlist exactly 2 of the 5 revenue archetypes that fit the value chain; reject the other 3 on record with a one-line reason before unit economics.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to SaaS subscription for any digital product; forcing explicit rejection of the other archetypes surfaces hidden assumptions.

rule.statement: Always compute LTV with a lifetime cap of 60 months; the formula 1/churn explodes at low churn rates and produces fantasy LTVs.
rule.rationale: 1% monthly churn implies 8.3-year average lifetime; SaaS products rarely sustain customers that long without 10-K cohort evidence.

rule.statement: Run unit economics in three scenarios (P10 pessimistic, P50 base, P90 optimistic) before writing any verdict; the P10 verdict is the only one that matters for runway decisions.
rule.rationale: Single-point LTV:CAC hides the distribution; operators need to know if the model survives a bad quarter, not just a median one.

rule.statement: The model passes only if it survives all five stress tests (Scale, Competition, Churn, CAC, Time-to-profit) at P50 and does not go below 1:1 LTV:CAC at P10.
rule.rationale: A model that passes only 4 of 5 has a known failure mode that materializes as the business scales; documenting it beats optimism.

rule.statement: Refuse to compute LTV with monthly churn below 1% unless backed by a public 10-K; sub-1% churn is a unicorn claim requiring evidence.
rule.rationale: Agents accept 0.5%/month churn from blog posts; LTV explodes 5x and the model looks viable when it is not.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
